features.description: Locate the failing code accessing PullRequest.head attribute
features.details: Search the automation codebase for code that accesses the 'head' attribute on a PullRequest object. Look for patterns like 'pull_request.head' or similar. Identify the exact file(s) and line numbers where this occurs.

features.description: Research correct PyGithub PullRequest API usage
features.details: Review the PyGithub library documentation or GitHub API documentation to understand the correct way to access pull request head information. In PyGithub, the correct access pattern is typically through properties like 'head.ref', 'head.sha', or 'head.repo'. Document the correct API usage.

features.description: Fix the incorrect PullRequest attribute access
features.details: Update the code to use the correct attribute access pattern for the PullRequest object. Ensure the fix handles the head information properly according to the PyGithub API. Add appropriate null checks if needed.

features.description: Add unit tests for PullRequest attribute access
features.details: Create unit tests that verify the correct access of PullRequest attributes, including the head property. Use mocking to simulate GitHub API responses. Ensure the tests cover edge cases like missing or null head values.
After deploying the fix, manually trigger or simulate the processing of Issue #115 to verify the automation now works correctly. Monitor for any additional errors.
Once the fix is verified, close Issue #117 with a summary of the changes made. Update any relevant documentation about the automation system's PullRequest handling.

overall_approach: Investigate the automation codebase to locate the incorrect attribute access on the PullRequest object, understand the correct GitHub API/PyGithub library usage, implement the fix, and add tests to prevent regression.
potential_risks: The automation code may have multiple incorrect attribute accesses that need fixing
Changes to the automation system could affect other workflows
The original issue #115 may need to be re-processed after the fix
There may be version compatibility issues with the GitHub API client library
